tossups.question: A sculptural self-portrait by this artist shows her in a free-flowing gown and standing on top of a lotus flower while making Buddhist mudra gestures with her hands. Another sculpture by this artist shows a man wearing only a loose robe, with his eyes closed and arms outstretched. This artist of Chinoise(“sheen-WAHZ”) and Washington’s Titanic Memorial wears a blue jacket and bright turquoise pants while reclining on a sofa in a portrait by Robert Henri (“HEN-rye”). An institution named for this woman was the original occupant of a Marcel Breuer (“BROY-er”)-designed building whose trapezoidal windows jut out from its exterior. In 2017, an institution named for this woman controversially featured Dana Schutz’s Open Casket, which depicts Emmett Till, in a biennial exhibition of contemporary American art. For 10 points, name this member of the Vanderbilt family who founded a namesake New York museum.
tossups.answer: Gertrude Vanderbilt Whitney [accept Gertrude Vanderbilt before “Vanderbilt” is read; accept Whitney Museum of American Art; accept Whitney Biennial]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Gertrude Vanderbilt Whitney [accept Gertrude Vanderbilt before "Vanderbilt" is read; accept Whitney Museum of American Art; accept Whitney Biennial]
tossups.question_sanitized: A sculptural self-portrait by this artist shows her in a free-flowing gown and standing on top of a lotus flower while making Buddhist mudra gestures with her hands. Another sculpture by this artist shows a man wearing only a loose robe, with his eyes closed and arms outstretched. This artist of Chinoise("sheen-WAHZ") and Washington's Titanic Memorial wears a blue jacket and bright turquoise pants while reclining on a sofa in a portrait by Robert Henri ("HEN-rye"). An institution named for this woman was the original occupant of a Marcel Breuer ("BROY-er")-designed building whose trapezoidal windows jut out from its exterior. In 2017, an institution named for this woman controversially featured Dana Schutz's Open Casket, which depicts Emmett Till, in a biennial exhibition of contemporary American art. For 10 points, name this member of the Vanderbilt family who founded a namesake New York museum.

tossups.question: At one event in this institution, women but not men were permitted to use a special Wi-Fi router to buy land in the successor countries to Yugoslavia, an idea of Irena Haiduk. In conjunction with an event at this institution, Anicka Yi went to the Amazon Rainforest in search of the "Flavor Genome." In 2017, visitors to this institution could strap on VR goggles and witness Jordan Wolfson savagely beating a man to death with a baseball bat. For much of that same event at this institution, (*) Parker Bright physically protested Dana Schutz's appropriation of Emmett Till. A building originally constructed for this institution will soon be the temporary home of the Frick Collection. In 2015, this museum moved from a building designed by Marcel Breuer to a new facility in the West Village. For 10 points, name this New York museum that stages a noted biennial.
tossups.answer: Whitney Museum of American Art [or the Whitney; accept Whitney Biennial]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Whitney Museum of American Art [or the Whitney; accept Whitney Biennial]

In 2017, visitors to this institution could strap on VR goggles and witness Jordan Wolfson savagely beating a man to death with a baseball bat. For much of that same event at this institution, (*) Parker Bright physically protested Dana Schutz's appropriation of Emmett Till. A building originally constructed for this institution will soon be the temporary home of the Frick Collection. In 2015, this museum moved from a building designed by Marcel Breuer to a new facility in the West Village. For 10 points, name this New York museum that stages a noted biennial.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about Joseph Grigely (“GRIDGE-lee”), an artist who has been deaf since age 10, for 10 points each.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about Joseph Grigely ("GRIDGE-lee"), an artist who has been deaf since age 10, for 10 points each.
parts: In 2000, Grigely was forced to hire his own interpreter to participate in this New York City Museum of American Art’s namesake “biennial” exhibition.

Grigely critiqued this photographer’s exhibition “The Blind” for its use of upside-down Braille. In 1981, this artist worked as a maid in Venice to create the book The Hotel, which meticulously catalogs customers’ private belongings.

Grigely broke stone replicas of this sort of object on the walls of Mass MoCA in a piece from the 2024 show “In What Way Wham?”. This general sort of round feature is shaded pink in nearly every painting by Yue Minjun.
parts_sanitized: In 2000, Grigely was forced to hire his own interpreter to participate in this New York City Museum of American Art's namesake "biennial" exhibition.

Grigely critiqued this photographer's exhibition "The Blind" for its use of upside-down Braille. In 1981, this artist worked as a maid in Venice to create the book The Hotel, which meticulously catalogs customers' private belongings.

Grigely broke stone replicas of this sort of object on the walls of Mass MoCA in a piece from the 2024 show "In What Way Wham?". This general sort of round feature is shaded pink in nearly every painting by Yue Minjun.
answers: Whitney Museum of American Art [or The Whitney; or Whitney Biennial]
heads [accept faces; accept any answer referring to the artist’s own head or face; prompt on specific facial body parts like ears or lips] (The piece is “Between the Walls and Me.”)

bonuses.leadin: In 1967, this art dealer’s father co-founded Art Cologne, the oldest contemporary art fair in the world. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In 1967, this art dealer's father co-founded Art Cologne, the oldest contemporary art fair in the world. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this German art dealer whose empire now rivals Larry Gagosian’s. In 2023, he launched an LA gallery with an exhibition of photo-transfered paintings by the Nigerian artist Njideka (“in-jee-DAY-kuh”) Akunyili Crosby.
Zwirner’s gallery in Chelsea, Manhattan, is near this museum, which hosts a prominent biennial and was founded by a Vanderbilt family patron.

Zwirner also has an art gallery in this city, which hosts a museum of visual culture called M+ and the first White Cube gallery outside London. Norman Foster designed a bank in this city with exposed suspension trusses and maintenance cranes that look like cannons.

answers: Whitney Museum [or The Whitney; or Whitney Museum of American Art; accept Whitney Biennial; accept Gertrude Vanderbilt Whitney]
Hong Kong [or HK, Xiānggǎng, Hoeng gong, Hionggong, or Hēunggóng] (Foster designed the HSBC Building.)

packet.name: G. Prelims 7 - Texas A, Toronto A, Toronto B

bonuses.leadin: A bust of this man appears next to a woman wearing a blue turban in a portrait by George Caleb Bingham. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this man who was sculpted along with Samuel Kirkwood and Sequoyah by the self-taught artist Vinnie Ream for the US Capitol building. The Piccirilli (“PEE-chee-REE-lee”) Brothers helped a different artist create a statue of this man.
Late in her life, Vinnie Ream exhibited some of her sculptures in this city at the World’s Columbian Exposition.

This other sculptor was neighbors with Daniel Chester French when she created a memorial to the Titanic disaster in Washington, D.C. She later founded a namesake museum with the help of her secretary, Juliana Force.
answers: Abraham Lincoln (The second statue is by Daniel Chester French.)
Gertrude Vanderbilt Whitney [or Gertrude Vanderbilt Whitney] (She founded New York’s Whitney Museum.)
answers_sanitized: Abraham Lincoln (The second statue is by Daniel Chester French.)
Gertrude Vanderbilt Whitney [or Gertrude Vanderbilt Whitney] (She founded New York's Whitney Museum.)

bonuses.leadin: Kara Walker used this technique to create colored backgrounds for silhouette works like Darkytown Rebellion and Insurrection! (Our Tools Were Rudimentary, Yet We Pressed On). For 10 points each:
parts: Name this technique also used to create large images on monuments and buildings by Polish artist Krzysztof Wodiczko [shish-toff voh-DITCH-koh]. This technology has been used to turn the Empire State Building into a huge digital photo frame.

Jenny Holzer’s many public projections often use text from this series of hers, in which messages like “Protect me from what I want” and “Abuse of power comes as no surprise” were displayed on LED signs and billboards.

The activist group Decolonize This Place made extensive projections onto this museum’s façade during 2019 protests of its board. In 2017, this museum controversially displayed Dana Schutz’s painting Open Casket.

bonuses.leadin: Without modern art museums, there would be no place for the common man to view the art of the aforementioned painters. Answer some questions about them. For 10 points each:
parts: This museum in Manhattan showcases 20th and 21st century American art, such as the works of Edward Hopper, Stuart Davis, and Charles Demuth. Juliana Force helped collect the art for the museum’s namesake patron.
Some of the famous works that this British museum houses are George Braque’s The Guitarist, de Chirico’s The Uncertainty of the Poet, and Rodin’s The Kiss.
This museum in Tallinn explores Estonian art from pre-Soviet to post-Soviet eras. It was first housed in the Kadriorg Palace, and it contains major works by Johann Koler.

bonuses.leadin: This artist stacked black boxes filled with found objects like spindles and chair legs for her installation Sky Cathedral. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this Russian emigre who staged the solo exhibition Moon Garden + One, and was known for her monochrome wooden wall sculptures, like Dawn's Wedding Feast.
Many of Nevelson's sculptures are housed in this museum in New York City's Upper East Side, which hosts a biennial exhibition of up-and-coming artists.
While working as an assistant on his mural Man at the Crossroads, Nevelson began an affair with this Mexican muralist.
answers: Diego Rivera [accept, but axe-murder anyone who says, Diego Maria de la Concepcion Juan Nepomuceno Estanislao de la Rivera y Barrientos Acosta y Rodriguez]
answers_sanitized: Diego Rivera [accept, but axe-murder anyone who says, Diego Maria de la Concepcion Juan Nepomuceno Estanislao de la Rivera y Barrientos Acosta y Rodriguez]

bonuses.leadin: Name these American museums that have commissioned expansions from Renzo Piano, which narrows the options to every major museum in the United States. For 10 points each:
parts: Piano designed the Modern Wing of this museum in Grant Park with an extended sunshade made of aluminum that has been dubbed “The Flying Carpet”. Its collection includes Bedroom in Arles and Nighthawks.

This New York museum will move its entire collection from its current building on Madison Avenue by Marcel Breuer to a new, nine-story location in the Meatpacking District that Piano will fnish in 2015. This museum is best-known for its namesake “biennial” exhibition showcasing works by new American artists. 9

Piano created a “modernist” entrance building during his renovation of this New York museum whose original building was designed by Charles McKim. Its collection is renown for its illuminated manuscripts, including the metalwork covers of the Lindau Gospels.

bonuses.leadin: The two figures in this painting wear similar blue hats, which have led some scholars to suggest that they are doppelgängers. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painting of two women eating lunch at a the title restaurant. The restaurant's sign is visible hanging out the window to the right.
Chop Suey is by this American painter of urban scenes, such as the patrons of a diner up late in Nighthawks.
The majority of Hopper's works are housed in this museum in New York City on 75th Street and Madison Avenue, which specializes in contemporary American art, but which is not the MoMA.
